headline: TV Titan Ted Turner: A Legacy Beyond Breaking News
lede: Media revolutionary Ted Turner, who transformed global broadcasting and launched the 24-hour news cycle, has died at 87, leaving an indelible mark on entertainment and communication.
body:
## The Media Visionary's Transformative Journey

Ted Turner didn't just change television — he reimagined how the world would consume information and entertainment. When he launched CNN in 1980, skeptics dismissed the concept of round-the-clock news. Turner proved them spectacularly wrong.

### A Network Empire

Beyond news, Turner built a media constellation that reshaped entertainment landscapes. Cartoon Network brought animation to unprecedented audiences. Turner Classic Movies became a sanctuary for cinema history, preserving classic films for generations.

His entrepreneurial vision extended far beyond traditional broadcasting. The United Nations Foundation demonstrated Turner's commitment to global dialogue and humanitarian causes. He understood media as more than entertainment — as a powerful platform for communication and change.

### Cultural Impact

Turner's bold strategies often seemed radical. Colorizing classic black-and-white films sparked intense debates among cinema purists. His maverick approach challenged industry conventions, pushing boundaries of what media could accomplish.

His legacy transcends networks and programming. Turner fundamentally transformed how audiences worldwide consume information, creating templates for global media that continue influencing broadcasters today.
